failure_mode: commit_review silently skips thread files in mentions: that don't exist on disk — published post 2026-05-22-monitoring-is-a-depreciating-asset references cot-monitorability but no thread file was ever created, leaving /thread/cot-monitorability/ as 404 on the live site
suggested_fix: if a thread_slug in mentions has no corresponding file at projects/research/threads/<slug>.md, return ok:false with a clear error so the publish path aborts; OR auto-create a stub thread file from the article's slug/title so the publish always carries a renderable thread page

attempt_notes.note: added missing-thread guard to commit_review; fails loudly with named missing threads instead of silent skip

failure_mode: draft_review in-tick flow discovers work via draft_article list-threads (file-glob of threads/*.md) only; ripe organic arcs tracked as prose in working.md Active-threads are never materialized into thread files, so they are invisible to drafting and draft_review comes up empty every cycle while publishing stalls. Organic counterpart of the assignment path has no compose-thread step.
suggested_fix: Add a step 0 to the in-tick flow, before 'Run draft_article list-threads': read working.md Active threads; for any arc that clears the ripeness bar but has no projects/research/threads/<slug>.md file, open the thread file now per memory/thread-structure.md 'Proactive: ripe organic arc with no thread file'.
